justification: All pass criteria met: Both stakeholders' interests are characterized fairly and in detail. Formal escalation is produced explicitly recommending VP of Product or equivalent decision-maker. No acceptance criteria are produced (the array is empty). Confidence level is 35, well below the 45 threshold. Proposed compromises (Options C and D) are clearly labeled as recommendations requiring both teams to revisit their positions, not presented as resolutions.

observations.text: Excellent identification of the conflict as mutually exclusive and unresolvable at the engineering level
Both stakeholders' interests characterized with genuine empathy — neither dismissed or minimized
Formal escalation with explicit recommendation to escalate to VP of Product with both teams present
Empty acceptance_criteria and business_rules arrays correctly reflect that nothing can be finalized
Confidence level of 35 appropriately reflects the unresolved contradiction
Identified a critical missing stakeholder (Legal/Compliance) that neither team consulted — adds genuine analytical value beyond the scenario
Edge cases correctly note 'Cannot define without resolving the core conflict' rather than assuming a resolution
Options presented as a menu for decision-makers, not as a fait accompli
Rejection reasons clearly articulate why the spec cannot proceed as-is

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Real-time courier tracking feature for customer-facing delivery experience. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved at the engineering level.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Customer Experience","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courie
